text.title: Ollama Agent Integration — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that gate any application of the ollama-agent-integration methodology. Sources: Ollama documentation — OpenAI compatibility; LangChain Ollama integration docs; LlamaIndex Ollama LLM docs.

rule.statement: Use Ollama's OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions endpoint when the agent framework already supports OpenAI; only drop to /api/chat when you need ollama-native streaming or structured output.
rule.rationale: OpenAI-compat lets you reuse the framework's retry / streaming / tracing infrastructure without writing a custom adapter.

rule.statement: Pass tool definitions with explicit JSON Schemas (type, properties, required, additionalProperties=false); never inline tool docs into the system prompt.
rule.rationale: Quantised local models hallucinate tool arguments when schemas are inline prose; strict schemas plus JSON-mode parser repair are mandatory.

rule.statement: Wrap tool-call parsing in a one-shot JSON repair attempt (e.g. json-repair); only escalate to a human / route to a larger model if repair fails.
rule.rationale: Local models emit malformed JSON ~5–15% of the time; one repair pass recovers most and avoids burning calls on the cloud fallback.

rule.statement: Warm the model with a single short request at agent start (model load is up to 30 s for 70B quants); never let the first user-visible request pay the load cost.

rationale.source: Anthropic agent design patterns (transposed to local)

rule.rationale: Cold-start latency on local models is the dominant user-visible failure mode and is fully preventable.

rule.statement: Cap context to 80% of the model's stated max (e.g. 24k for a 32k-context model) and bin-pack tool results before each turn.
rule.rationale: Local quantised models silently truncate at the boundary, producing answers that ignore the most recent inputs without raising an error.
